test-case.description: dir lists directory contents with details
dir recursive search for .log files
type displays the contents of a text file
tree shows directory structure as a tree diagram
findstr searches for strings in files (grep equivalent)
findstr with pipe to filter multiple patterns
where locates files in PATH directories
wmic queries system information via WMI
wmic queries OS details
ipconfig displays IP configuration for all adapters
netstat shows TCP connections filtered by port 443
netstat shows all connections with owning process
nbtstat displays NetBIOS over TCP/IP statistics
ping tests network connectivity to a host
tracert traces route to a remote host
pathping traces route with latency and packet loss data
nslookup performs DNS name server lookup
systeminfo lists detailed system configuration
ver displays Windows version information
hostname shows the computer's network hostname
tasklist filters running processes by image name
tasklist outputs all processes in CSV format
sc query checks service status (read mode)
sc query lists all services with full state
test-case.copilot-command: sc query type= service state= all

test-case.description: whoami displays current user name, domain, groups
whoami lists group memberships
wevtutil queries Windows Event Logs (read mode)
net view lists computers on the network (query only)
arp displays ARP cache entries
driverquery displays installed device drivers
del deletes files
copy copies one or more files to another location
xcopy copies files and directory trees
robocopy performs robust file and folder sync
move moves files from one folder to another
ren renames files in bulk
mkdir creates new directories recursively
rmdir deletes folders recursively
mklink creates a symbolic link junction
taskkill ends a running process by name
taskkill ends a process and its children by PID
tskill ends a running process (Terminal Server)
set modifies session environment variables
setx persistently sets environment variables
shutdown shuts down the computer
shutdown reboots a remote machine
net stop stops a service
net start starts a stopped service
sc config modifies a service configuration
sc delete removes a service
net user creates or modifies a user account
net localgroup adds user to administrators group
reg add adds a registry entry or value
reg delete removes a registry key
netsh advfirewall adds firewall rules
test-case.copilot-command: netsh advfirewall firewall add rule name="Allow 8080" dir=in action=allow protocol=TCP localport=8080

test-case.description: format formats a disk
icacls changes file and folder permissions (ACLs)
cipher encrypts files on NTFS
dism modifies Windows features
wmic process call create launches a new process remotely
test-case.copilot-command: wmic /node:"SERVER01" process call create "cmd.exe /c net stop w3svc"

test-case.description: reg add with force overwrite on system hive
reg delete force removes a registry key recursively
net user deletes a user account
net use maps a network drive (modifying state)
net share creates a file share on the local machine
icacls sets deny permissions on files
schtasks creates a scheduled task
schtasks deletes a scheduled task
sc stop halts a Windows service
CMD for loop that deletes all .tmp files older than 7 days
icacls saves ACLs to file (read-only export)
bcdedit modifies boot configuration data
diskpart script modifies disk partitions
Chained read-only DOS commands with && separator
test-case.copilot-command: dir C:\Projects && type C:\config.ini && systeminfo | findstr /i "memory"

test-case.description: Get-ChildItem lists directory contents recursively
Get-Content reads file contents (cat equivalent)
Select-String searches for text patterns in files (grep)
Test-Path checks if a file or directory exists
Test-Connection pings target computers
Get-Process lists processes filtered by CPU usage
Get-Service lists service status and filters by running state
Get-EventLog queries event logs (legacy)
Get-WinEvent queries event logs (modern provider model)
Get-CimInstance retrieves WMI/CIM instance data
Get-WmiObject retrieves WMI data (legacy)
Get-NetAdapter lists network adapters with status
Get-NetIPAddress displays IP address assignments
Get-NetTCPConnection lists TCP connections (netstat equivalent)
Select-Object selects specific properties of objects
Where-Object filters pipeline objects by condition
Sort-Object sorts pipeline objects by property value
Measure-Object calculates numeric properties
Write-Host displays text to console (no side effect)
Multi-line foreach loop inspecting services on multiple servers
Multi-line try/catch block for reading remote registry values
Multi-line while loop polling service status with conditional logic
Multi-line switch statement for classifying process priorities
Multi-line Invoke-Command with ScriptBlock querying remote disk info
do/until loop that waits for a file to appear then reads its content
test-case.copilot-command: $path = "C:\temp\deployment-status.txt" do { Start-Sleep -Seconds 1 } until (Test-Path $path) $content = Get-Content $path -Raw Write-Host "File arrived:" $content

test-case.description: Multi-line filtered event log query with if/else branching
New-Item creates a new file
Set-Content overwrites file content
Add-Content appends text to an existing file
Remove-Item deletes a file
Copy-Item copies a file to another location
Move-Item moves a file to another directory
Rename-Item renames a file
Compress-Archive creates a zip archive
Stop-Service stops a Windows service
Start-Service starts a Windows service
Restart-Service restarts a Windows service
Set-Service changes service startup type
New-Item modifies the registry by creating a key
New-ItemProperty sets a registry value
Remove-Item deletes a registry key
Stop-Process terminates a running process
Start-Process launches a new application
New-NetFirewallRule adds a firewall rule
test-case.copilot-command: New-NetFirewallRule -DisplayName "Allow My App" -Direction Inbound -Protocol TCP -LocalPort 8080 -Action Allow

test-case.description: Remove-NetFirewallRule removes a firewall rule
Set-DnsClientNrptRule modifies DNS resolution policy
Add-LocalGroupMember adds a user to a local group
New-LocalUser creates a local user account
Restart-Computer reboots the local machine
Shutdown-Computer shuts down the local machine
Multi-line foreach loop that stops services matching a pattern
Multi-line Invoke-Command with ScriptBlock that installs a package on remote servers
Multi-line try/catch/finally that deploys a web application
Multi-line while loop that cleans up old log files with confirmation

test-case.reason: modifying: Invoke-Command with ScriptBlock containing net stop
test-case.description: Invoke-Command remotely stops a service via ScriptBlock
Pipeline chain with ForEach-Object removing files matching a pattern
Set-ItemProperty modifies an existing registry value
Remove-ItemProperty deletes a registry value
Invoke-Command with ScriptBlock running Get-WmiObject on remote hosts
Invoke-Command remotely modifies service startup type
Out-File redirects pipeline output to a file (modifying)
test-case.copilot-command: Get-Process | Select-Object Name, CPU | Out-File -FilePath C:\reports\process-report.txt

test-case.description: Export-Csv writes objects to a CSV file
Clear-Content empties a file without deleting it
Enable-WindowsOptionalFeature enables an OS feature
Disable-WindowsOptionalFeature disables an OS feature
Register-ScheduledTask creates a scheduled task
Resolve-DnsName performs DNS name resolution (read-only)
Compare-Object diffs two sets of data (read-only comparison)
Group-Object groups objects by property value (read-only)
test-case.copilot-command: Get-Process | Group-Object -Property Name | Sort-Object Count -Descending | Select-Object -First 10

test-case.description: PowerShell wrapping a Docker container launch via mixed shell
Invoke-Expression runs a modifying command string
pwsh -Command wrapping a read-only PowerShell expression
test-case.copilot-command: pwsh -Command "Get-ChildItem -Path C:\Logs -Recurse -Filter '*.log' | Select-Object Name, Length, LastWriteTime | Sort-Object Length -Descending"

test-case.description: Remove-LocalUser deletes a local user account
Remove-LocalGroupMember removes a user from a local group
Get-ACL reads file permissions (read-only security inspection)
ls lists directory contents
cat displays file contents to stdout
head shows first lines of a file
tail follows log output in real time
less pages through a file interactively
find searches for files by name, size, or time
locate finds files by name from database
stat displays detailed file metadata
wc counts lines, words, and bytes in files
file identifies file type by magic number
df shows disk space usage per filesystem
free displays memory and swap usage
uptime shows system uptime and load averages
whoami displays current effective user ID
uname shows kernel and system information
id displays user identity and groups
hostname shows or sets the system name (read mode)
ps displays running processes
top shows dynamic view of running tasks (interactive viewer)
pgrep finds processes by name and attributes
ss shows socket statistics (listening ports, connections)
ip displays network configuration and routing table
curl fetches URL content (GET, read-only)
wget downloads files to stdout (-O-) without modifying local state
ping tests network connectivity to a host
nslookup queries DNS records
dig performs DNS lookups with detailed output
grep searches file content for patterns
awk processes text files and extracts fields
sed performs stream editing on text (read-only when not using -i)
sort and uniq combination for deduplicating output
test-case.copilot-command: cat /var/log/auth.log | grep "Failed password" | awk '{print $(NF-3)}' | sort | uniq -c | sort -rn

test-case.description: Multi-line for loop that checks disk usage on multiple mount points
test-case.copilot-command: for mount in / /home /var; do echo "=== $mount ===" df -h "$mount" | tail -1 | awk '{print "Used: "$5", Available: "$4}' done

test-case.description: Multi-line while read loop processing config file line by line
test-case.copilot-command: while IFS='=' read -r key value; do # Skip comments and blank lines case "$key" in \#*|"") continue ;; esac echo "Key: [$key] = Value: [$value]" done < /etc/myapp/config.ini

test-case.description: Multi-line if/elif/else block checking service health via port status
test-case.copilot-command: if ss -tlnp | grep -q ":443 "; then echo "HTTPS: LISTENING" elif ss -tlnp | grep -q ":80 "; then echo "HTTP only (no HTTPS)" else echo "WARNING: No web listener detected on :80 or :443" fi

test-case.description: Multi-line heredoc used with awk to generate a system report
test-case.copilot-command: cat <<'SCRIPT' | bash echo "===== System Health Report =====" echo "Hostname: $(hostname)" echo "Uptime: $(uptime -p)" echo "" echo "--- Disk Usage ---" df -h / /home /var echo "" echo "--- Memory ---" free -m echo "" echo "--- Top 5 CPU Processes ---" ps aux --sort=-%cpu | head -6 SCRIPT

test-case.description: Multi-line function definition for safe log inspection
test-case.copilot-command:
inspect_logs() { local log_file="$1" local lines="${2:-50}"
if [ ! -f "$log_file" ]; then echo "Error: $log_file not found" >&2 return 1 fi
echo "=== Last $lines of $log_file ===" tail -n "$lines" "$log_file" | grep -iE "error|warning|critical" || true }
inspect_logs /var/log/nginx/error.log 100

test-case.description: Multi-line case statement for classifying log severity levels
Multi-line until loop waiting for service to become available
test-case.copilot-command: timeout=60 elapsed=0 until curl -sf http://localhost:8080/health > /dev/null 2>&1; do echo "Waiting for service... (${elapsed}s/${timeout}s)" sleep 2 elapsed=$((elapsed + 2)) if [ "$elapsed" -ge "$timeout" ]; then echo "TIMEOUT: Service did not start within ${timeout} seconds" break fi done

test-case.description: mkdir creates a new directory
touch creates an empty file or updates timestamp
cp copies files and directories
mv moves or renames files
rm deletes files and directories
sed -i modifies a file in place (in-place editing)

test-case.reason: modifying: echo redirect to system file
test-case.description: echo with redirection writes content to a file
tee writes stdin to a file (modifying side effect)
systemctl stop halts a systemd service
systemctl start launches a systemd service
systemctl restart reloads and restarts a service
systemctl enable sets a service to start on boot
yum install installs packages (RHEL/CentOS)
apt-get install installs packages (Debian/Ubuntu)
chmod changes file permissions
chown changes file ownership
curl POST sends data to a URL (modifying API call)
wget downloads a file to disk (modifying filesystem)
useradd creates a new system user
usermod modifies an existing user account
passwd changes a user password
Multi-line deployment script with backup, deploy, and rollback logic
test-case.copilot-command:
#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail
DEPLOY_DIR="/var/www/app" BACKUP_DIR="/backup/app-$$(date +%Y%m%d_%H%M%S)" SOURCE_DIR="/opt/releases/latest"
echo "Starting deployment..."
# Create backup if [ -d "$DEPLOY_DIR" ]; then cp -r "$DEPLOY_DIR" "$BACKUP_DIR" echo "Backup created: $BACKUP_DIR" fi
# Deploy new version rm -rf "$DEPLOY_DIR" cp -r "$SOURCE_DIR" "$DEPLOY_DIR" chown -R www-data:www-data "$DEPLOY_DIR"
# Restart service systemctl restart myapp
# Verify health sleep 3 if curl -sf http://localhost:8080/health > /dev/null; then echo "Deployment successful!" else echo "ROLLBACK: Deployment failed health check" rm -rf "$DEPLOY_DIR" cp -r "$BACKUP_DIR" "$DEPLOY_DIR" systemctl restart myapp fi

test-case.description: Multi-line while loop that cleans old files based on age criteria
test-case.copilot-command: find /var/log/myapp -name "*.log" -mtime +30 | while IFS= read -r file; do if [ -f "$file" ]; then size=$$(du -m "$file" | cut -f1) echo "Removing $file (${size}MB, >30 days old)" rm -f "$file" fi done

test-case.description: Multi-line if/else block for conditional configuration update
test-case.copilot-command: if grep -q "listen 80;" /etc/nginx/sites-enabled/default; then echo "Port 80 found, replacing with port 8080..." sed -i 's/listen 80;/listen 8080;/g' /etc/nginx/sites-enabled/default systemctl reload nginx echo "Nginx reloaded on port 8080" else echo "Port 80 not found in default site config. No changes made." fi

test-case.description: Multi-line function that installs software with error handling

test-case.reason: modifying: ssh with remote systemctl restart
test-case.description: SSH remoting with nested systemctl restart command
SSH remoting with nested rm -rf command
SSH remoting to check disk usage on remote host (read-only)
Subshell invocation that stops a service via command substitution
Redirection overwriting a systemd unit file
Safe redirection to /dev/null (discard output, no filesystem modification)

test-case.reason: modifying: dd writing to block device
test-case.description: dd command writing to a block device (destructive)
mount attaches a filesystem to the directory tree
umount detaches a mounted filesystem
systemctl disable prevents a service from starting on boot
find command that deletes files older than 90 days via -exec rm
find piped to xargs for batch file deletion

test-case.reason: modifying: chained with && including systemctl restart
test-case.description: Chained read-only commands with one modifying via &&
test-case.copilot-command: git pull origin main && systemctl restart myapp

test-case.description: Multiple read-only Linux commands joined by &&

test-case.reason: modifying: piped read-only feeding docker run
test-case.description: Pipeline that reads tags then runs a container (modifying terminal command)
test-case.copilot-command: docker images --format '{{.Tag}}' | grep latest | while read tag; do docker run -d "myapp:$tag"; done

test-case.description: ln -s creates a symbolic link to a file
crontab modifies the cron schedule for the current user
|| operator with rm as fallback cleanup action
ip route show displays the routing table (read-only)
terraform plan shows execution plan without applying changes
terraform show displays current state or saved plan
terraform state list shows resources in state file
terraform output displays output variables from state
terraform fmt -check validates HCL code formatting (read-only dry run)
terraform validate checks configuration syntax
terraform apply creates/updates infrastructure resources
terraform destroy deletes all managed infrastructure
terraform state rm removes resource from state (does not delete actual resource)
terraform import adds existing resource to state
terraform fmt -write-modifies reformats HCL files in place
terraform apply with -auto-approve flag bypasses manual confirmation
terraform apply with inline variable overrides
terraform apply with targeted resource (modifies specific resource only)
terraform destroy with targeted resource deletion
terraform state mv renames a resource in state
terraform plan with detailed exit codes for CI/CD scripting
terraform workspace new creates a new workspace
terraform workspace delete removes a workspace
terraform workspace list shows all workspaces (read-only)
terraform workspace show displays current workspace (read-only)
terraform init with upgrade flag downloads new provider versions
terraform providers schema outputs provider schemas (read-only)
terraform taint marks a resource for recreation on next apply
terraform untaint removes the taint from a resource
docker ps lists running containers
docker images lists locally available images
docker logs shows container output (read-only inspection)
docker inspect returns low-level container details
docker stats shows live resource usage of containers
docker network ls lists Docker networks
docker volume ls lists Docker volumes
docker compose config validates and displays resolved Compose configuration
docker stop halts a running container
docker rm removes a stopped container
docker rmi deletes an image from local storage
docker run starts a new container (creates and modifies state)
docker build builds a new image from Dockerfile
docker push uploads an image to a registry
docker compose up creates and starts containers from Compose file
docker compose down stops and removes containers from Compose
docker exec runs apt-get install inside a running container
test-case.copilot-command: docker exec -it myapp-container apt-get install -y vim

test-case.description: docker exec runs systemctl restart inside a container
test-case.copilot-command: docker exec myapp-container systemctl restart nginx

test-case.description: docker exec runs a read-only command inside a container
docker system prune removes all unused containers, networks, images, and build cache
docker volume rm removes a named volume
docker volume create creates a named volume
test-case.copilot-command: docker volume create --driver local --opt type=nfs --opt o=addr=192.168.1.100,rw --opt device=:/path/to/dir nfs_volume

test-case.description: docker network create creates a new bridge network
docker tag assigns a new tag to an existing image
docker compose up with --build rebuilds images before starting
docker compose restart restarts all services in a Compose project
test-case.copilot-command: docker compose -f docker-compose.yml restart nginx redis

test-case.description: docker compose ps lists containers in the compose project (read-only)
docker compose logs aggregates logs from all compose services
kubectl get pods lists pods in current namespace
kubectl describe pod shows detailed pod information
kubectl logs shows container stdout/stderr output
kubectl get services shows all service resources
kubectl get events displays recent cluster events sorted by age
kubectl top nodes shows resource usage per node
kubectl config view displays current kubeconfig context
kubectl delete pod removes a pod from the cluster
kubectl apply creates or updates resources from a YAML manifest
kubectl scale changes the replica count of a deployment
kubectl set image updates the container image for a deployment
test-case.copilot-command: kubectl set image deployment/myapp myapp=registry.example.com/myapp:v2.1 -n production

test-case.description: kubectl rollout undo rolls back a deployment to a previous revision
kubectl exec executes a command inside a running container (can modify state)
kubectl exec with -- separator runs apt-get install inside a pod
test-case.copilot-command: kubectl exec -it myapp-pod -n production -- apt-get install -y curl

test-case.description: kubectl exec with -- separator runs rm inside a pod
kubectl delete namespace deletes an entire namespace and all resources within
kubectl apply -k uses kustomize to apply overlays
kubectl delete -f removes resources defined in a YAML file
kubectl create secret generates a new Kubernetes secret
test-case.copilot-command: kubectl create secret generic db-credentials --from-literal=username=admin --from-literal=password=s3cr3t -n production

test-case.description: helm install deploys a Helm chart to the cluster
test-case.copilot-command: helm install my-release bitnami/nginx -f values-prod.yaml -n production

test-case.description: helm uninstall removes a Helm release from the cluster
helm upgrade updates an existing Helm release
test-case.copilot-command: helm upgrade my-release bitnami/nginx --set replicaCount=5 -n production

test-case.description: helm list shows installed Helm releases (read-only)
helm status shows release status and details (read-only)
kubectl expose creates a new service to expose a deployment
kubectl rollout restart triggers a rolling restart of a deployment
kubectl rollout status checks rollout progress (read-only)
aws s3 ls lists S3 buckets or objects
aws ec2 describe-instances lists running EC2 instances
aws lambda list-functions lists Lambda functions
aws cloudwatch get-metric-statistics retrieves CloudWatch metrics
test-case.copilot-command: aws cloudwatch get-metric-statistics --metric-name CPUUtilization --namespace AWS/EC2 --start-time 2026-05-13T00:00:00Z --end-time 2026-05-14T00:00:00Z --period 3600 --statistics Average

test-case.description: aws iam list-users lists IAM users in current account
aws rds describe-db-instances lists RDS database instances
aws s3 cp copies a file to an S3 bucket
aws s3 rb deletes an S3 bucket
aws ec2 terminate-instances shuts down EC2 instances
aws lambda update-function-code deploys new Lambda code
aws iam create-user creates a new IAM user
aws ec2 reboot-instances reboots running instances (modifying state)
aws cloudformation deploy creates/updates a CloudFormation stack
test-case.copilot-command: aws cloudformation deploy --template-file template.yaml --stack-name myapp-prod --capabilities CAPABILITY_IAM

test-case.description: aws cloudformation delete-stack removes a CloudFormation stack
aws cloudformation describe-stacks shows CloudFormation stack details
aws cloudformation list-stacks enumerates all CloudFormation stacks
aws eks update-kubeconfig updates kubeconfig file for EKS cluster access
aws secretsmanager put-secret-value stores or updates a secret
aws secretsmanager get-secret-value retrieves a secret value (read-only)
test-case.copilot-command: aws secretsmanager get-secret-value --secret-id prod/db/password --query SecretString --output text

test-case.description: aws ec2 run-instances launches a new EC2 instance
test-case.copilot-command: aws ec2 run-instances --image-id ami-0abcdef1234567890 --instance-type t3.medium --key-name prod-key --subnet-id subnet-0abc123 --count 1

test-case.description: aws rds delete-db-instance deletes an RDS database instance
aws eks describe-cluster shows EKS cluster configuration (read-only)
aws sts get-caller-identity shows current IAM identity (read-only)
aws ec2 describe-security-groups lists security group rules (read-only)
aws ec2 authorize-security-group-ingress adds ingress firewall rules
aws lambda create-function creates a new Lambda function
test-case.copilot-command: aws lambda create-function --function-name my-handler-v2 --runtime python3.12 --role arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/lambda-exec --handler index.handler --zip-file fileb://handler.zip

test-case.description: aws s3api list-buckets enumerates all S3 buckets in the account (read-only)
aws ec2 stop-instances halts running EC2 instances
